task.title: Implement post-commit CodeWiki GC capability
task.summary: Add a source-owned GC application/tool path that classifies and purges eligible CodeWiki artifacts only after archive commit proof and restore-ledger emission.

goal.outcome: Agents and adapters can invoke a safe CodeWiki GC dry-run/purge capability instead of manually deleting tracked `.codewiki` artifacts.
acceptance: GC dry-run reports eligible tracked builds/validation/runtime artifacts without deleting them.
Tracked purge requires archive commit SHA/tree proof and refuses to purge without it.
Tracked purge writes a restore ledger with removed paths and `git restore --source=<archive-sha> -- <path>` commands before deletion.
GC does not purge fail/block/current-policy/current-publication validation reports or active unconsumed handoffs.
Ignored runtime/session handoffs can be cleaned under runtime policy with clear reporting.
Pi adapter exposes the GC capability through a typed tool schema.
Main CodeWiki skill names agent ownership of `.codewiki` GC and the archive commit -> GC commit protocol.
Implementation/task-close output rules require post-commit GC review or explicit defer/block evidence after close/publication commit proof.
Validation/publication guidance distinguishes GC ledger proof from validation/content proof and forbids pre-commit tracked purge.
Tool references include the GC capability and restore-ledger safety fields.
Prompt asset tests assert GC enforcement text appears in relevant skill assets.
non_goals: Do not change task-close validation policy except where needed to require GC safety inputs.
Do not push, publish, or remove remote history.
Do not make generated graph state the source of purge truth.
Do not implement GC deletion logic in skill text.
Do not create container/umbrella roadmap tasks.
Do not weaken fresh validation or task-close gates.
verification: Run stale-reference/alignment/changed audits for touched docs and source.
Run npm run test:smoke and npm run typecheck.

delta.desired: Additional outcome: Agents see post-commit CodeWiki GC as a mandatory end-of-flow invariant rather than optional cleanup.
